question: I want to evaluate ASR on oncology dictation. We've seen it mishear chemotherapy drug names. Where do I start?
ground_truth: The agent invokes clinical-asr-build, asks 1-2 specialty-aware clarifying questions, and walks the user through Stage 2 — term curation with entity_category, the inlined LLM sentence-generation prompt, the two-tier IPA tagging pipeline, then QA-mode synthesis before the full Cartesian product. Magpie TTS is called via the inlined NVCF recipe (function ID 877104f7-..., grpc.nvcf.nvidia.com:443). KER is named as the headline metric for Stage 3.
expected_behavior: Asked at least one specialty-aware clarifying question
Proposed candidate terms with entity_category set
Mentioned the two-tier IPA strategy (override → merriam-webster → magpie_g2p)
Referenced the Magpie TTS NVCF function ID 877104f7-... or the inlined recipe — NOT a /read-aloud or /riva-tts skill call
Mentioned the QA-mode approval gate before running the full Cartesian product
Did NOT route the user to /read-aloud, /riva-tts, or /data-designer

question: Magpie is mispronouncing 'esomeprazole' even with the IPA override. What do I check?
ground_truth: Verify the IPA passes Magpie's en-US phoneme inventory; verify the SSML wrapping is syntactically valid (single-word vs multi-word handling differs — multi-word terms need per-word IPA components with matching word counts). If both check out, it's a Magpie bug — file with the Riva team.
expected_behavior: Suggested verifying the IPA candidate against Magpie's en-US phoneme inventory
Mentioned the multi-word IPA word-count match requirement (term-words == ipa-words)
Acknowledged that persistent mispronunciation despite verified IPA is a Magpie/Riva team bug to file externally
Did NOT recommend fine-tuning as the fix for a single mispronunciation

question: I don't have an LLM endpoint available. Can I still build a manifest?
ground_truth: Yes — use the template fallback documented in Step 2b. Sentences are canned but the rest of the cycle (IPA tagging, QA, full Cartesian) proceeds normally. Tag rows so a future cycle can regenerate.
expected_behavior: Recognized that the LLM step has a template fallback (Plan B)
Showed the user the template fallback approach with mechanical interpolation per context_type
Mentioned tagging the rows so they can be regenerated later when an LLM is available
Did NOT route the user to /data-designer
